citations.narrative:
The licensee did not have accurate records concerning animals other than dogs and cats that he has acquired and are on
hand at the time of inspection. These animals include a pair of ring-neck doves, 1 hedgehog, 1 tenrec, 3 rabbits, 1 Indian
peacock, 1 domestic chicken, 8 domestic ducks, 1 domestic turkey, and 5 domestic geese. The records of acquisition are
missing the following:
- Name and address of the person from whom the animals were acquired,
- The USDA license numbers (if licensed), and
- The vehicle license number and State, and driver's license number/photo ID (if not licensed).
Accurate recordkeeping including complete information on where regulated animals are acquired is essential to ensure
appropriate regulatory oversight of all animals used for regulated activities. Correct by keeping and maintaining complete
information listed in this subsection for all animals acquired for regulated purposes, including names, addresses, USDA or
driver's and vehicle license numbers, dates, species, and number of animals.

The enclosure housing 1 guinea pig contains a shelf of bare, untreated wood for climbing/resting. Untreated and unsealed
wood is not substantially impervious to moisture and cannot be readily sanitized. Disease agents may soak into the wood
and may pose a risk of illness to the guinea pig. Correct by ensuring all surfaces in the primary enclosures for guinea pigs
are substantially impervious to liquids and moisture.
This is a 1st Re-License inspection for a Class “C” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the
applicant.
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspections or by July 12, 2026, or the applicant will forfeit the license fee
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection.
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
n

narrative:
2.75(b)(1) 
   
Records: Dealers and exhibitors. 
The licensee did not have accurate records concerning animals other than dogs and cats that he has acquired and are on 
hand at the time of inspection. These animals include a pair of ring-neck doves, 1 hedgehog, 1 tenrec, 3 rabbits, 1 Indian 
peacock, 1 domestic chicken, 8 domestic ducks, 1 domestic turkey, and 5 domestic geese. The records of acquisition are 
missing the following: 
- Name and address of the person from whom the animals were acquired, 
- The USDA license numbers (if licensed), and 
- The vehicle license number and State, and driver's license number/photo ID (if not licensed). 
 
Accurate recordkeeping including complete information on where regulated animals are acquired is essential to ensure 
appropriate regulatory oversight of all animals used for regulated activities. Correct by keeping and maintaining complete 
information listed in this subsection for all animals acquired for regulated purposes, including names, addresses, USDA or 
driver's and vehicle license numbers, dates, species, and number of animals. 
3.28(a)(1) 
   
Primary enclosures. 
The enclosure housing 1 guinea pig contains a shelf of bare, untreated wood for climbing/resting. Untreated and unsealed 
wood is not substantially impervious to moisture and cannot be readily sanitized. Disease agents may soak into the wood 
and may pose a risk of illness to the guinea pig. Correct by ensuring all surfaces in the primary enclosures for guinea pigs 
are substantially impervious to liquids and moisture. 
 
This is a 1st Re-License inspection for a Class “C” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the 
applicant. 
 
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspections or by July 12, 2026, or the applicant will forfeit the license fee 
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection. 
 
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
